entities.name: CHICKPEA

entities.description: The time from sowing to the appearance of flowers

entities.name: SEED SIZE

entities.description: The timing of biological events such as flowering, podding, and maturity

entities.name: PODDING
entities.description: The stage of pod formation in the plant

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The stage at which the plant has completed its growth cycle

entities.description: Environments with a limited growing window

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of seed sprouting

entities.name: SEEDLING VIGOUR
entities.description: The health and strength of a germinating seedling

entities.name: SEEDLING MASS
entities.description: The weight of the seedling

entities.description: A small-seeded kabuli chickpea genotype

entities.description: A chickpea genotype used in crossing

entities.description: A chickpea genotype used in crossing

entities.name: INHERITANCE
entities.description: The process by which genetic traits are passed from parents to offspring

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced per unit area or plant

entities.name: SEGREGATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of how alleles are distributed in offspring generations

entities.name: DUPLICATE RECESSIVE EPISTASIS
entities.description: A type of gene interaction where two recessive genes mask the expression of a trait

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of 100 seeds, used as a measure of seed size

entities.name: YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: The amount of seed yield produced by a single plant

entities.name: NUMBER OF PODS PER PLANT

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: The total biomass produced by a single plant

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total biological yield

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant

entities.name: SEED YIELD IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The goal of increasing the amount of seed produced
